title: Limiting Reagent and Percent Yield Calculator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Interactive stoichiometry tool that determines the limiting reagent, theoretical yield, and percent yield for common reaction pairs with animated visuals.

learningObjectives: Identify the limiting reagent by comparing moles-per-coefficient for two reactants.
Calculate theoretical yield and percent yield given reactant masses and actual product mass.

keyQuestions: Which reactant limits the reaction and how do you know?
How does the actual yield compare to the theoretical limit?
commonMisconceptions: The reactant with the smaller mass is always limiting.
Percent yield can exceed 100% without experimental error.
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture the Show Work log and annotate each step.
Pairs explain how altering a reactant mass shifts the limiting reagent.
